architecture.summary: A neural embedding system for ALC (Attributive Language with Complements) knowledge base concepts. Concept embeddings are learned such that complex concepts can be compositionally constructed from their parts using neural operators (neural constructors). A shared neural reasoner head, trained to perform approximate subsumption checking, operates on these embeddings across different knowledge bases without retraining, enabled by projecting all concept embeddings into a shared vector space.

biblio.title: Reason-able embeddings: Learning concept embeddings with a transferable neural reasoner

claims.statement: Embeddings reflect the semantics of ALC concepts such that an embedding of a complex concept can be computed from the embeddings of its parts by using appropriate neural constructors
A shared reasoner head enables approximate subsumption checking for arbitrarily complex concepts across all knowledge bases without per-ontology retraining
The difference in reasoning performance between training a separate reasoner head for each ontology and using a shared reasoner head is negligible
